problems.question: I think he is a very intelligent person, (　　) many people don't agree with me.
problems.ja: 彼はとても知的な人だと私は思うが，多くの人は私に同意しない。
problems.en: I think he is a very intelligent person, but many people don't agree with me.

problems.question: I have never been to the country, (　　) do I want to go there in the future.
problems.ja: 私はその国に今まで行ったことはないし，将来そこに行きたいとも思わない。
problems.en: I have never been to the country, nor do I want to go there in the future.

problems.question: We sometimes have strong differences of opinion, (　　) we remain close friends.
problems.ja: ときどきは意見が大きく異なることもあるが，それでも私たちが親しい友人であることに変わりはない。
problems.en: We sometimes have strong differences of opinion, yet we remain close friends.

problems.question: Make haste, (　　) you will miss the last train.
problems.ja: 急ぎなさい。さもないと，終電に乗り遅れますよ。
problems.en: Make haste, or you will miss the last train.

problems.question: Think success, (　　) you are more likely to succeed.
problems.ja: 成功を思い描きなさい。そうすれば，より成功する可能性が高まります。
problems.en: Think success, and you are more likely to succeed.

problems.question: We have stayed at both good (　　) bad camping sites.
problems.ja: 私たちは，良いキャンプ場と悪いキャンプ場の両方に泊まったことがある。
problems.en: We have stayed at both good and bad camping sites.

problems.question:
太郎は雨だから家に帰ったのではなく，疲れていたから家に戻ったのです。
Taro returned home (not because / it / but / he / was rainy / was tired / because).
problems.rawAnswer: not because it was rainy but because he was tired
problems.ja: 太郎は雨だから家に帰ったのではなく，疲れていたから家に戻ったのです。
problems.answer: not because it was rainy but because he was tired
problems.en: Taro returned home not because it was rainy but because he was tired.

problems.question:
人間は，言葉だけでなく，表情によっても意思を伝達するものだ。
People (also / but / communicate / expressions / facial / languages / not / only / with / with).
problems.rawAnswer: communicate not only with languages but also with facial expressions
problems.ja: 人間は，言葉だけでなく，表情によっても意思を伝達するものだ。
problems.answer: communicate not only with languages but also with facial expressions
problems.en: People communicate not only with languages but also with facial expressions.

problems.question: We will (　　) go to the movies or go to a baseball game Friday night.
problems.ja: 金曜の夜，私たちは映画か野球の試合を見に行きます。
problems.en: We will either go to the movies or go to a baseball game Friday night.

problems.question:
(a) Mike doesn't like coffee.  He doesn't like tea, either.
(b) Mike likes (　　) coffee (　　) tea.
problems.ja:
(a) マイクはコーヒーが好きではない。紅茶も好きではない。
(b) マイクはコーヒーも紅茶も好きではない。
problems.en: Mike likes neither, nor coffee neither, nor tea.

problems.question: (　　) it took only three months to finish the project is an amazing achievement.
problems.ja: そのプロジェクトを終えるのにたった３か月しかかからなかったということは，驚くべき成果だ。
problems.en: That it took only three months to finish the project is an amazing achievement.

problems.question: Many scientists are researching the theory (　　) global warming is caused by human activity.
problems.ja: 地球温暖化は人間の活動によって引き起こされるという理論を，多くの科学者たちが検証しているところだ。
problems.en: Many scientists are researching the theory that global warming is caused by human activity.

problems.question: It is often assumed that humans differ from other creatures (　　) they can think and speak.
problems.ja: 考えたり話したりできるという点で，人間は他の生物とは違うと，しばしば考えられている。
problems.en: It is often assumed that humans differ from other creatures in that they can think and speak.

problems.question: We know nothing about him (　　) he is a good teacher.
problems.ja: よい教師であるということを除いて，私たちは彼について何も知らない。
problems.en: We know nothing about him except that he is a good teacher.

problems.question: Bob asked me (　　) I felt well.
problems.ja: ボブは私に体調がいいかどうか尋ねた。
problems.en: Bob asked me if I felt well.

problems.question: (　　) he is famous doesn't matter.
problems.ja: 彼が有名かどうかなど，どうでもいいことだ。
problems.en: Whether he is famous doesn't matter.

problems.question: The situation at work got better (　　) the new boss arrived.
problems.ja: 新しい上司が来て，職場の状況が良くなった。
problems.en: The situation at work got better when the new boss arrived.

problems.question: I have been living in Nagoya (　　) I came to Japan six years ago.
problems.ja: ６年前に日本に来てから，私はずっと名古屋に住んでいる。
problems.en: I have been living in Nagoya since I came to Japan six years ago.

problems.question: You had better write the number down in the notebook (　　) you forget it.
problems.ja: 忘れないうちにノートにその番号を書きとめておいた方がいいよ。
problems.en: You had better write the number down in the notebook before you forget it.

problems.question:
彼の息子が薬局を引き継ぐのもそう遠くないでしょう。
(takes / won't / his son / before / it / be / over / long) the drugstore.
problems.rawAnswer: It won't be long before his son takes over
problems.ja: 彼の息子が薬局を引き継ぐのもそう遠くないでしょう。
problems.answer: It won't be long before his son takes over
problems.en: It won't be long before his son takes over the drugstore.

problems.question:
私は初めて彼を見たとき，彼を学生だと思った。
I (student / saw / time / a / the / I / took / for / him / first) him.
problems.rawAnswer: took him for a student the first time I saw
problems.ja: 私は初めて彼を見たとき，彼を学生だと思った。
problems.answer: took him for a student the first time I saw
problems.en: I took him for a student the first time I saw him.

problems.question: You will never enjoy watching the game (　　) you learn the rules.
problems.ja: ルールを覚えるまで，あなたはその試合を見て楽しむことはないだろう。
problems.en: You will never enjoy watching the game until you learn the rules.

problems.question: The exam had started (　　) I got to class.
problems.ja: 私が教室に着いたときには，試験は始まっていた。
problems.en: The exam had started by the time I got to class.

problems.question:
(a) On arriving at the hotel, he called his son.
(b) (　　) (　　) (　　) he arrived at the hotel, he called his son.
problems.ja: (a) (b) ホテルに着くとすぐ，彼は息子に電話をかけた。
problems.en: As soon as he arrived at the hotel, he called his son.

problems.question:
テレビをつけたとたん，おかしいなと思った。
I recognized something strange about the (I / it / moment / on / switched / television / the).
problems.rawAnswer: television the moment I switched it on
problems.ja: テレビをつけたとたん，おかしいなと思った。
problems.answer: television the moment I switched it on
problems.en: I recognized something strange about the television the moment I switched it on.

problems.question: I had no sooner sat down to read the newspaper (　　) the telephone rang.
problems.ja: 新聞を読もうと座ったとたん，電話が鳴った。
problems.en: I had no sooner sat down to read the newspaper than the telephone rang.

problems.question:
その車が角を曲がるやいなや，車輪が外れた。
No (the car / the corner / had / sooner / than / turned) a wheel came off.
problems.rawAnswer: sooner had the car turned the corner than
problems.ja: その車が角を曲がるやいなや，車輪が外れた。
problems.answer: sooner had the car turned the corner than
problems.en: No sooner had the car turned the corner than a wheel came off.

problems.question: I had (　　) gone to bed when the telephone rang.
problems.ja: ベッドに入ったとたん，電話が鳴った。
problems.en: I had hardly gone to bed when the telephone rang.

problems.question: (　　) the sun rises in the east, Japan has long been called "the land of the rising sun."
problems.ja: 太陽は東から昇るため，日本は長い間「日出づる国」と呼ばれてきた。
problems.en: Since the sun rises in the east, Japan has long been called "the land of the rising sun."

problems.question: He will not come to the party (　　) he has so many assignments to do by tomorrow morning.
problems.ja: 明朝までにやらなければならない宿題が非常に多くあるので，彼はパーティーには来ないだろう。
problems.en: He will not come to the party because he has so many assignments to do by tomorrow morning.

problems.question: Many exam candidates lose marks simply (　　) they do not read the questions properly.
problems.ja: きちんと設問を読まないという理由だけで，失点する受験者も大勢いる。
problems.en: Many exam candidates lose marks simply because they do not read the questions properly.

problems.question:
本に書いてあるからといって，何でもすぐに信用してはいけない。
Don't believe everything (in books / it's / because / immediately / just / written).
problems.rawAnswer: immediately just because it's written in books
problems.ja: 本に書いてあるからといって，何でもすぐに信用してはいけない。
problems.answer: immediately just because it's written in books
problems.en: Don't believe everything immediately just because it's written in books.

problems.question: (　　) the country has a new democratic government, the people will at last have more freedom.
problems.ja: その国には，今や新たな民主主義政府があるのだから，国民はより多くの自由をついに手にすることだろう。
problems.en: Now that the country has a new democratic government, the people will at last have more freedom.

problems.question: Helen jogs every morning (　　) she is too tired.
problems.ja: ひどく疲れていない限り［ひどく疲れていなければ］，ヘレンは毎朝ジョギングをする。
problems.en: Helen jogs every morning unless she is too tired.

problems.question: You can go ahead and use any book you like on the shelves, (　　) you leave all your bags in the lockers.
problems.ja: ロッカーにカバンをすべて置いたら，先へ進んで棚にあるあなたの好きなどんな本でも利用することができます。
problems.en: You can go ahead and use any book you like on the shelves, provided you leave all your bags in the lockers.

problems.question: (　　) we miss the train, what shall we do?
problems.ja: もし電車に乗り遅れたら，どうしよう。
problems.en: Supposing we miss the train, what shall we do?

problems.question: (　　) you were President, what would you do?
problems.ja: もしあなたが大統領だったら，何をしますか。
problems.en: Suppose you were President, what would you do?

problems.question: They'll lend us their flat <u>on condition that</u> we look after it.
problems.ja: 留守を預かるという条件で，彼らはアパートを貸してくれます。
problems.en: They'll lend us their flat on condition that we look after it.

problems.question: At Emi's birthday party he behaved (　　) he were a child.
problems.ja: エミの誕生日パーティーで，彼はまるで子どものようにふるまった。
problems.en: At Emi's birthday party he behaved as if he were a child.

problems.question: Mathew looks very tired. He looks (　　) he hasn't slept for days.
problems.ja: マシューはとても疲れているようだ。何日も眠っていないかのように見える。
problems.en: Mathew looks very tired. He looks as though he hasn't slept for days.

problems.question: (　　) I learned how to hold the club properly, my golf game improved a lot.
problems.ja: ひとたびクラブの正しい持ち方を学んだら，私のゴルフの腕はかなり上がった。
problems.en: Once I learned how to hold the club properly, my golf game improved a lot.

problems.question: I like the restaurant we went to last night, (　　) it was a little too expensive.
problems.ja: 少し値段が高すぎたけれども，昨晩行ったレストランは良かった。
problems.en: I like the restaurant we went to last night, although it was a little too expensive.

problems.question: It is important not to give out your credit card number to anyone over the phone (　　) you know who you are talking to.
problems.ja: たとえ話し相手がだれか知っていても，電話ではクレジットカード番号をだれにも教えないことが大事です。
problems.en: It is important not to give out your credit card number to anyone over the phone even if you know who you are talking to.

problems.question: (　　) a vast amount of research has been conducted, there is still a great deal that is not known about the workings of the human brain.
problems.ja: 膨大な量の研究がなされてきたけれども，人間の脳の働きにはまだ未知の部分がたくさんある。
problems.en: Even though a vast amount of research has been conducted, there is still a great deal that is not known about the workings of the human brain.

problems.question: Sleepy (　　) he was, he kept on studying for the examination.
problems.ja: 彼は眠かったけれども，テストの勉強を続けた。
problems.en: Sleepy as he was, he kept on studying for the examination.

problems.question: (　　) they agree or not, I will carry out the plan.
problems.ja: 彼らが賛成しようがしまいが，私はその計画を実行する。
problems.en: Whether they agree or not, I will carry out the plan.

problems.question: The movie was (　　) interesting that I wanted to see it again.
problems.ja: その映画はとてもおもしろかったので，私はもう一度見たいと思った。
problems.en: The movie was so interesting that I wanted to see it again.

problems.question: It was (　　) a fine day that they went hiking.
problems.ja: とても良い天気だったので，彼らはハイキングに出かけた。
problems.en: It was such a fine day that they went hiking.

problems.question: (　　) was the force of the explosion that all the windows broke into pieces.
problems.ja: 爆発の威力がとてもすごかったので，窓はすべて粉々に砕けた。
problems.en: Such was the force of the explosion that all the windows broke into pieces.

problems.question: I overslept, (　　) I was late for the meeting.
problems.ja: 寝坊をした。それで会議に遅れてしまった。
problems.en: I overslept, so I was late for the meeting.

problems.question: She wanted to publish her poetry (　　) everyone could read it.
problems.ja: 彼女はだれもが読めるように，自分の詩集を出版したいと思っていた。
problems.en: She wanted to publish her poetry so that everyone could read it.

problems.question:
■英文の意味に最も近いものを，①～④から一つ選べ。
You need to speak up so everyone can hear you.
problems.ja:
みんなに聞こえるようにあなたは大声で話す必要がある。
①あなたが言うことをみんな聞く準備ができている。
②あなたの言うことにみんなが賛成するとは限らない。
③あなたはもっと大声で話さなければならない。さもないと，人々は聞き取ることができないだろう。
④他のみんなの考えることにあなたは耳を傾けるべきだ。
choices: Everybody is ready to listen to what you are going to say.
Not everybody is going to agree with what you say.
You have to talk louder, otherwise people will not be able to hear.
You should listen to what everyone else thinks.
problems.en: You need to speak up so everyone can hear you.

problems.question: He got to the station early for (　　) that he might miss the last train.
problems.ja: 最終電車を逃さないように，彼は早めに駅に着いた。
problems.en: He got to the station early for fear that he might miss the last train.

problems.question: You should write down Satsuki's number (　　) you forget it.
problems.ja: 忘れた場合に備えて，サツキの電話番号を書きとめておくべきです。
problems.en: You should write down Satsuki's number in case you forget it.

problems.question: A desert stretched on all sides (　　) the eye could reach.
problems.ja: 見渡す限り，あらゆる方角に砂漠が広がっていた。
problems.en: A desert stretched on all sides as far as the eye could reach.

problems.question: Any bread will do as (　　) as it's fresh.
problems.ja: 焼きたてでありさえすれば，どんなパンでもいいですよ。
problems.en: Any bread will do as long as it's fresh.

problems.question: I went swimming almost every day (　　) I stayed in Okinawa.
problems.ja: 沖縄滞在中は，ほぼ毎日泳ぎに行った。
problems.en: I went swimming almost every day while I stayed in Okinawa.

problems.question: Some people say that the Internet is a passing fashion, (　　) others believe it is a turning point in human history.
problems.ja: インターネットは一時の流行だと言う人もいるが，一方で，人類の歴史における転換点だと考える人もいる。
problems.en: Some people say that the Internet is a passing fashion, while others believe it is a turning point in human history.

problems.question: (　　) Joe agreed with Mary for the most part, there were still some points that he could not accept.
problems.ja: ジョーはほとんどの部分でメアリーに同意したが，受け入れることができないところがまだいくつかあった。
problems.en: While Joe agreed with Mary for the most part, there were still some points that he could not accept.

problems.question:
年がいくと，だんだん骨がもろくなるらしい。
They say our bones get weaker and weaker (　　) we get older.
problems.ja: 年がいくと，だんだん骨がもろくなるらしい。
problems.en: They say our bones get weaker and weaker as we get older.

problems.question: The students did it (　　) they were told to do it.
problems.ja: 生徒たちはそれを言われるようにやった。
problems.en: The students did it as they were told to do it.

problems.question:
私たちはあるがままに物事を受け入れなければなりません。
(accept / they / we / are / have / things / as / to).
problems.rawAnswer: We have to accept things as they are
problems.ja: 私たちはあるがままに物事を受け入れなければなりません。
problems.answer: We have to accept things as they are
problems.en: We have to accept things as they are.
